project.name: HVAC OpsForge (hvac-ops-agent)
project.primary_purpose: Multi-agent operations co-pilot for HVAC and trade service teams. Turns reactive ops data into an executive Dispatch Baseline with human-in-the-loop approval.
project.phase: Phase 1 demo-ready with synthetic fallback + optional Live Mongo. Core agent loop exists but specialist implementations are inconsistent post-split.

tech_stack.agents: Custom BaseAgent hierarchy + dynamic SPECIALISTS registry
tech_stack.data: MongoDB (pymongo) with pure Pydantic models + synthetic/demo_data.json fallback
tech_stack.llm: OpenAI (LeadArchitect only; most specialists are rule/heuristic based)

entry_points.name: Streamlit Dashboard
entry_points.description: Primary demo UI. Routes to three views via sidebar radio. Initializes session state, injects styles, then renders Dispatch Workspace / Technician Field App / Financial Ops (AR).

entry_points.name: FastAPI Jobs API
entry_points.description: Background job submission. POST /api/jobs accepts goals + optional project_path, queues run_pm_job. In-memory job store (not production Redis).

entry_points.name: CLI
entry_points.description: forge_pm command runs full orchestrator with progress. modernize/analyze are mostly legacy CodeForge stubs.

presentation.app.py: Streamlit entry. Page config, state init, navigation router.

views/.dispatch_workspace.py: Executive dashboard. Goals editor, Live Mongo toggle, Execute button → utils.engine.execute_pm_run → tabs for Overview/Requirements/Risks/Schedule/Agent Logic + exports (ZIP, QuickBooks XLSX).
views/.technician_view.py: Field-facing mobile-ish view for prioritized jobs, parts checklist, risks, offline JSON export.
views/.ar_operations.py: AR review queue with approve/reject decisions.

ui/.charts.py: Risk bar chart + Gantt as PNG bytes.

application.utils/engine.py: Controller for Streamlit. live_mongo → async run_pm_job with progress; else → hardcoded synthetic result (does NOT call full agent chain).
application.core/orchestrator.py: run_pm_job(). LeadArchitect → dynamic SPECIALISTS registry dispatch → assemble_dispatch_baseline → optional human approval → execute_approved_actions (mostly logging).
application.core/dispatch_baseline.py: Flagship output. JTBD requirements register, Porter's Five Forces risk ranking, AR priority queue, parts queue, ROI summary, Markdown/JSON reports, QuickBooks CSV/XLSX mappers.

domain_agents.core/agents/lead_architect.py: Only agent with real LLM. Ingests project files or synthetic, builds execution_plan (or fallback).

core/agents/specialists/.__init__.py: SPECIALISTS dict + @register_specialist decorator. Imports the individual modules to trigger registration.
core/agents/specialists/.inventory_forecaster.py: STUB — class body is only 'pass'. Non-functional.
core/agents/specialists/.risk_assessor.py: STUB — class body is only 'pass'. Non-functional.
core/agents/specialists/.ar_collector.py: Partial — Pydantic ARResult, mongodb_tools, cashflow_score, optional parts combination.
core/agents/specialists/.parts_availability_checker.py: Most complete package agent — JobPartsRequest validation, low inventory query, ReorderRecommendation list, availability_score.

domain_agents.core/agents/specialists.py: LEGACY monolithic file containing the full working implementations of InventoryForecasterAgent, RiskAssessorAgent, SchedulerOptimizerAgent, ARCollectorAgent. Still imported by dispatch_baseline.py. This is currently the working code for inventory/risk/scheduler; the package split is incomplete.

data.core/tools/mongodb_tools.py: MongoDBTools singleton. Pure Pydantic read paths (JobDocument, InventoryItem, Invoice). Always falls back to memory/demo_data.json then hardcoded synthetic. upsert_job_state(PMJobState).
data.core/models/parts_schemas.py: Canonical models: InventoryItem, JobDocument, Invoice, ReorderRecommendation, JobPartsRequest, PartsAvailabilityResult, ARResult, ScheduleOptimizationResult, PMJobState.

happy_path_streamlit_live: User sets goals + enables Live Mongo
_resolve_agent_sequence() maps plan specialists via aliases → SPECIALISTS registry lookup
assemble_dispatch_baseline() normalizes into requirements / risks / schedule / AR / ROI / actions
Result stored in session_state['pm_result'] → UI tabs + charts + downloads
synthetic_path: Live Mongo off → _build_synthetic_result() returns static dict (no real agents run). This is the demo that actually works today.
approval_loop: _execute_approved_actions() only logs + builds execution_results; no real side effects yet

agent_roster.name: inventory_forecaster
agent_roster.status: broken in registry (stub); works only via legacy specialists.py

known_gaps_and_debt: Specialist package split incomplete: inventory_forecaster and risk_assessor are pure stubs. Registry points at them → orchestrator produces empty/failed results for those agents on the dynamic path.
dispatch_baseline.py still imports old classes from core.agents (legacy specialists.py). Dual import paths = drift.
README.md contains unresolved git merge conflict markers.
utils/engine.py synthetic path is a static mock, not a real agent run.
Human approval is status-only; no real action execution or write-back beyond logging.
PROJECT_MEMORY.md documents pytest collection failures and that Owner ROI Simulator was mock-only.
No auth, no multi-tenant, in-memory job store only.
recommended_next_agent_actions: 1. Finish the specialist split: move full logic from specialists.py into the package modules (especially inventory + risk) so SPECIALISTS registry is the single source of truth. Delete or deprecate the monolithic file.
2. Make execute_pm_run synthetic path actually call run_pm_job(use_mongo=False) so demos exercise real agents.
3. Resolve README merge conflict and rewrite as single coherent Phase-1 doc.
4. Add a simple approval path (Streamlit confirm or API endpoint) that calls _execute_approved_actions and writes back via mongodb_tools.
5. Fix or skip broken tests; get pytest green before new features.
6. Replace CodeForge leftovers in config.yaml and cli modernize stubs with HVAC OpsForge language.
